questionText: Prove that the sum of any two odd numbers is even.

steps.explanation: Every odd number is one more than an even number, so it has the form $2a+1$. Using a letter keeps the argument general.
The second odd number is independent of the first, so it needs its own letter $b$; writing it as $2a+1$ would wrongly force the two to be equal.
The claim is about adding the two odd numbers, so we write their sum.
Adding removes the brackets and gathers the two constant $1$s into a $2$.
Pulling out a factor of $2$ is the decisive move: it displays the sum explicitly as twice something.

steps.description: Identify the bracket as an integer.
steps.explanation: Since $a$ and $b$ are integers, so is $a+b+1$; hence the sum is $2 \times (\text{integer})$.
A number equal to $2$ times an integer is even by definition, so the sum of any two odd numbers is even.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: $(2a+1)+(2b+1)=2a+2b+2=2(a+b+1)$, which is $2\times$ an integer, hence even. $\blacksquare$
